# Decoder

> Open-source code understanding for the AI era. Read, review and audit code written by Copilot, Cursor, Claude Code and your team. BYOK cloud or fully local with Ollama / LM Studio.

Decoder is a non-profit, open-source project that helps developers, students and teams make sense of AI-generated code. It runs against your own provider keys (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, OpenRouter) or fully locally via Ollama and LM Studio. No vendor lock-in, no telemetry, MIT licensed.

## Pages

- [Home](/): What Decoder is, who it is for, supported providers.
- [Manifesto](/manifesto): Principles — open source, privacy-first, BYOK, local-first, multilingual.
- [Documentation](/docs): How to use Decoder — BYOK cloud, local Ollama/LM Studio, security and ownership.
- [How to review AI-generated code](/docs/how-to-review-ai-code): Guide to auditing code produced by Copilot, Cursor, Claude Code and other AI tools.
- [Open source](/open-source): License, contributing, repository.
- [Contributors](/contributors): Live list of contributors and merged pull requests from GitHub.
- [Terms](/terms): MIT License, permitted uses, trademark restrictions, disclaimer.
- [Privacy](/privacy): What data is stored, how it is protected, GDPR notes.
